  "findings": [
    "Vacuum decay test failed",
    "Micron level rose from 425 to 1495 microns in 10 minutes",
    "Indicates moisture in the system or a small leak"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Continue evacuation to boil off remaining moisture",
    "Perform a pressure test with dry nitrogen if the pressure continues to rise past atmospheric levels"
  ],
  "rationale": "The micron level rose from 425 to 1495 microns during a 10-minute standing decay test. A successful evacuation must hold below 500 microns (or at least below 1000 microns depending on manufacturer specifications, without rising rapidly). A rise of over 1000 microns in 10 minutes indicates that moisture is still boiling off in the system or there is a small leak.",
  "references": [
    "EPA Section 608",
    "Industry Standard Evacuation Procedures (500 micron hold)"
  ]
}
